subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus: The tide removes sand from Sandy Point Beach at a rate modeled by the function $R$, given by $R(t) = 2 + 5\sin\!\left(\tfrac{4\pi t}{25}\right)$ cubic yards per hour. A pumping station adds sand to the beach at a rate modeled by the function $S$, given by $S(t) = \dfrac{15t}{1 + 3t}$ cubic yards per hour. Both $R(t)$ and $S(t)$ have domain $0 \le t \le 6$.

parts.prompt: How much sand will the tide remove from the beach during this 6-hour period? Indicate units of measure.
rubric_criteria: How much sand will the tide remove from the beach during this 6-hour period? Indicate units of measure.

parts.prompt: Write an expression for $Y(t)$, the total number of cubic yards of sand on the beach at time $t$.
rubric_criteria: Write an expression for $Y(t)$, the total number of cubic yards of sand on the beach at time $t$.

parts.prompt: Find the rate at which the total amount of sand on the beach is changing at time $t = 4$.
rubric_criteria: Find the rate at which the total amount of sand on the beach is changing at time $t = 4$.

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 6$, at what time $t$ is the amount of sand on the beach a minimum? What is the minimum value? Justify your answers.
rubric_criteria: For $0 \le t \le 6$, at what time $t$ is the amount of sand on the beach a minimum? What is the minimum value? Justify your answers.
